title: poa cockayneana
intro: Poa cockayneana, also known as Cockayne's grass, is a species of grass that is native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Poa cockayneana are often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
